Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and promotes appreciation of cultural diversity (Target 4.7) by teaching students to "understand Indonesian cultures."
evidence: understand Indonesian cultures and the life of Indonesian people

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course promotes social inclusion (Target 10.2) by teaching about "Ethnicities in Indonesia," fostering understanding and respect for diversity.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Tangentially relates to safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4) through discussions on "popular public places in Indonesia" and cultural notes.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: By fostering cross-cultural understanding and an "international perspective," the course indirectly supports the promotion of peaceful societies.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity for international partnership (Target 17.9) by teaching language and culture, which are foundational for cross-border communication and knowledge sharing.
evidence: To possess basic language proficiency and application skills

gemini_critique.critique: Model A offered more comprehensive interpretations for Reduced Inequalities and Partnerships, which I've incorporated. My initial analysis was too conservative on these.
